codeReview.summary: Commit 3db38b2 adequately fixes the prior leading-dash flag-injection blocker from `fix-git-ops-checkout-redact`: `checkoutRef()` and `createTag()` now reject leading-dash refs before command construction, the new `InvalidRefError` is exported through the public API, and regression coverage matches the feature requirements. I found no new blocking issues, but one nearby comment still overstates what shell quoting alone guarantees.

issues.description: The new `assertValidRef()` guard at line 368 is what actually neutralizes the prior leading-dash injection issue, but the adjacent comment still says single-quoting itself prevents flag injection (`leading dashes inside single quotes are literal, not flags`). That security claim is inaccurate because shell quotes are stripped before `git` parses argv; correcting the comment would better document why this fix is safe.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill guidance still does not match how this git-ops fix rerun was actually performed or reported: the worker edited source before adding the new regression tests, skipped the documented manual-verification step, and the handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true` with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:28-30` requires writing tests before implementation and `:80-82` documents manual verification for SSH-related features. The transcript skeleton for worker session `9d34cb54-ca6e-4298-a220-51704b75f247` shows baseline tests, then edits to `packages/git-ops/src/errors.ts`, `src/index.ts`, `src/types.ts`, and `src/live.ts`, followed later by test edits/creation (`packages/git-ops/test/security.test.ts`, `packages/git-ops/test/leading-dash.test.ts`). The handoff `/home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/handoffs/2026-03-15T20-36-24-308Z__fix-git-ops-leading-dash__9d34cb54-ca6e-4298-a220-51704b75f247.json:16-26,138-140` records only automated validators and still marks `followedProcedure` true with an empty deviations list.

summary: Pass. The fix clears the prior blocker by rejecting leading-dash refs in `checkoutRef()` and `createTag()` with `InvalidRefError` before they can reach the git CLI, and the added regression coverage matches the requested `--detach`/`-b`-style cases. One non-blocking issue remains: a `checkoutRef()` comment still incorrectly credits shell quoting with preventing flag injection even though safety now depends on the new pre-validation guard.
